programs.name: Medi-Cal Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs pay Medicare Part B premiums and cost-sharing for low-income beneficiaries; QMB covers premiums, deductibles, coinsurance; SLMB and QI cover premiums only. Administered via Medi-Cal for over 1.7 million dually eligible Californians.[2][5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries; specific income thresholds (e.g., under A&D FPL limits like $1,835 individual).[2][5][6]
programs.benefit_value: Part B premium ($174.70/month in 2024, varies); QMB full cost-sharing relief; essential for affordability.[2][5]

programs.name: Multipurpose Senior Services Program Waiver (MSSP)
programs.what_it_does: The MSSP waiver provides in-home supports like home modifications, personal emergency response, homemaker services, and personal care assistance to help seniors avoid institutionalization. It also assists with transitioning from nursing homes back to the community.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors aged 65+ eligible for nursing home care under Medi-Cal with income up to $1,836/month and assets up to $130,000.[1]
programs.benefit_value: In-home services and supports to enable community living; part of Medi-Cal HCBS with no specific dollar value listed, tied to LTC eligibility.[1]

programs.name: Community Based Adult Services (CBAS)
programs.what_it_does: CBAS offers daytime care and supervision in certified adult day health care centers. It supports seniors needing assistance with daily activities in approximately 28 California counties.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible seniors and disabled individuals requiring daytime supervision and care.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Daytime care services; available through Medi-Cal without quantified annual value in sources.[1]

programs.name: CalFresh
programs.what_it_does: CalFresh is California's SNAP program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to buy groceries. It helps low-income seniors afford nutritious food.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ with income typically under 200% FPL; automatic eligibility for SSI recipients in CA.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per person for eligible seniors; max benefits scale by household size.

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help with heating/cooling bills and energy crises. California administers it for low-income households including seniors.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households at or below 150% FPL, prioritizing seniors 60+, disabled.[3]
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-$1,000+ for energy bills; crisis assistance up to utility shutoff prevention.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs. Administered in CA for low-income homes.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households below 200% FPL, prioritizing seniors, disabled, children.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued $5,000-$10,000+ per household, long-term energy savings.

programs.name: Health Insurance Counseling and Advocacy Program (HICAP)
programs.what_it_does: HICAP offers free, confidential one-on-one Medicare counseling, education, and advocacy. It assists with Medicare options, appeals, and Medi-Cal coordination.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries of any age, soon-to-be eligible, especially low-income seniors in CA.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling; helps access $1,000s in savings via proper enrollment.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Older Americans Act / CA Dept. of Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home-delivered nutritious meals to homebound seniors. Coordinates with local Area Agencies on Aging in California.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, low-income prioritized.[3]

programs.name: Multipurpose Senior Services Program (MSSP) Caregiver Support
programs.what_it_does: MSSP includes respite and caregiver support services as part of in-home assistance. Additional CA Family Caregiver Support Program offers respite, counseling.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of Medi-Cal eligible seniors 65+ at risk of nursing home placement.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Respite hours, training; in-home services valued via Medi-Cal rates.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: SCSEP provides part-time community service jobs with training for older workers. California hosts multiple grantees like Experience Works.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/wk, ~$8,000/year), leading to unsubsidized employment.

programs.name: California Senior Legal Services (via Legal Services Inc. / Area Agencies)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal aid for issues like housing, benefits, abuse. Funded through CA Dept. of Aging and federal Older Americans Act.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ unable to afford private attorneys.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation; protects benefits worth $10,000s.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Administered statewide by CA Dept. of Aging.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, board & care, assisted living in California.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints; improves facility conditions for 100,000+ seniors.

programs.name: State Supplementary Payment (SSP)
programs.what_it_does: SSP provides additional monthly cash payments to eligible SSI recipients to supplement federal benefits for basic living expenses like food and housing. It is administered by California to boost the total SSI payment amount beyond the federal level.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ receiving federal SSI.
programs.whats_unique: California offers one of the highest state supplements to federal SSI in the nation, with maximum combined payments reaching up to 89% of the federal poverty level as of 2020.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $943.72/month combined federal + state (2020 max); varies by living situation and year.

programs.name: Property Tax Postponement Program
programs.what_it_does: This program allows eligible seniors to defer property taxes on their primary residence until the home is sold or transferred. The deferred taxes plus interest are repaid from the property's proceeds at that time.
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners aged 62+ with household income ≤ $49,220 (2024) and at least 40% ownership.
programs.whats_unique: Unique deferral mechanism postpones taxes indefinitely without immediate payment burden, specific to California law.
programs.benefit_value: Defers 100% of property taxes; interest accrues at 7% annually.

programs.name: In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS)
programs.what_it_does: IHSS provides in-home personal care, domestic services, and supportive tasks to help seniors remain safely in their homes. Services are authorized based on assessed needs and delivered by approved providers.
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible seniors 65+ or disabled adults needing assistance to avoid institutionalization.
programs.whats_unique: California's large-scale state-funded program employs over 600,000 caregivers, one of the most extensive Medicaid waiver home care systems in the U.S.
programs.benefit_value: Up to 283 hours/month of services; average $15-20/hour wage for providers (varies by county).

programs.name: Caregiver Resource Centers (CRC)
programs.what_it_does: CRCs offer free or low-cost support including counseling, respite care, training, support groups, and care planning for family caregivers. Services address emotional, financial, and practical challenges of caregiving.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's, stroke, Parkinson's, or similar conditions; accessible statewide.
programs.whats_unique: California is the first state with a comprehensive statewide network of caregiver support centers available to every resident.
programs.benefit_value: Free services including respite (up to 20-40 hours/month in some cases) and counseling.

programs.name: Community-Based Adult Services (CBAS)
programs.what_it_does: CBAS delivers therapeutic adult day care including nursing, physical therapy, meals, and social activities. It supports medical needs and promotes independence for frail seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible seniors 65+ or adults 18-64 with disabilities meeting nursing facility level of care.
programs.whats_unique: California-specific Medi-Cal benefit providing structured daytime services as an alternative to institutional care.
programs.benefit_value: Up to 5 days/week attendance; full coverage for eligible participants.
